Attorney General Bonta Announces Settlement with DoorDash for Driver Misclassification Under AB 5

March 22, 2026

Attorney General Rob Bonta today announced a $43 million settlement with DoorDash, Inc. resolving claims that the gig delivery platform misclassified California drivers as independent contractors, denying them minimum wage, overtime, sick leave, and other workplace protections required under California Labor Code Section 2775 (AG 5).

Attorney General Bonta Files Lawsuit Against Comcast Corporation for Deceptive Billing Practices

March 7, 2026

California Attorney General Rob Bonta today filed suit against Comcast Corporation, alleging the cable provider engaged in false advertising and deceptive business practices by charging California consumers undisclosed fees and "broadcast TV surcharges" not included in advertised prices, affecting more than 2 million subscribers.

Attorney General Bonta Settles with Chevron Corporation for Air Quality Violations at Richmond Refinery

February 19, 2026

Attorney General Rob Bonta today announced a settlement requiring Chevron Corporation to pay $7.5 million and implement major emissions controls at its Richmond refinery, resolving allegations that the company exceeded permitted air pollution limits over a five-year period in violation of California environmental law.
